---
title: "Phase 6 — Radiation"
---

> 🚧 **Under Construction** — This phase is outlined and will be developed in full. The structure and reading map below guide independent study in the meantime.

## Overview

A uniformly moving charge produces a static or quasi-static field that just follows the charge around. But an *accelerating* charge radiates — it sends energy out to infinity in the form of electromagnetic waves that never come back. This is one of the most important phenomena in classical physics, and its analysis requires the full machinery of electrodynamics developed in Phase 4.

Phase 6 begins with the retarded potential solution for moving charges, derives the Liénard-Wiechert fields, and then analyzes the radiation patterns and power output for various types of accelerated motion.

## Topics

- **Retarded potentials revisited**: Jefimenko's equations; causality and the retarded time
- **The Liénard-Wiechert potentials**: exact fields of a point charge in arbitrary motion
- **Fields of a moving charge**: velocity fields (bound, falls off as $r^{-2}$) and radiation fields (falls off as $r^{-1}$)
- **Radiation from point charges**: the Larmor formula $P = \mu_0 q^2 a^2/(6\pi c)$; angular distribution $dP/d\Omega$
- **Relativistic generalization**: Liénard formula; relativistic beaming
- **Electric dipole radiation**: $P = \mu_0\ddot{p}^2/(6\pi c)$; angular distribution; connection to antenna theory
- **Magnetic dipole and electric quadrupole radiation**: higher multipoles and their suppression factors
- **Synchrotron radiation**: relativistic circular motion; characteristic frequency; angular and spectral distribution
- **Čerenkov radiation**: radiation from a charge moving faster than light *in a medium*
- **Radiation reaction**: the Abraham-Lorentz force; runaway solutions; the self-force problem

## Reading Map

| Topic | Griffiths | Zangwill | Boas |
|-------|-----------|----------|------|
| Retarded potentials, Jefimenko | Ch. 10 §1–2 | Ch. 20 §1–3 | — |
| Liénard-Wiechert potentials | Ch. 10 §3 | Ch. 20 §4–6 | — |
| Larmor formula | Ch. 11 §2 | Ch. 21 §1–2 | — |
| Dipole radiation | Ch. 11 §1 | Ch. 21 §3–5 | — |
| Synchrotron, Čerenkov | — | Ch. 21 §6–8; Ch. 23 §1–3 | — |
| Radiation reaction | Ch. 11 §3 | Ch. 23 §4–6 | — |

## Key Equations

Larmor formula (non-relativistic):
$$
P = \frac{\mu_0 q^2 a^2}{6\pi c} = \frac{q^2 a^2}{6\pi\epsilon_0 c^3}
$$

Electric dipole radiation power:
$$
P = \frac{\mu_0 \ddot{p}^2}{6\pi c}
$$

Liénard formula (relativistic Larmor):
$$
P = \frac{\mu_0 q^2 \gamma^6}{6\pi c}\!\left(a^2 - \left|\frac{\mathbf{v}}{c}\times\mathbf{a}\right|^2\right)
$$
